user_message: Just came out of competitive positioning with Pacific Northwest Outfitters. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - Pacific Northwest Outfitters
Date: 2024-01-26
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), David (Pacific Northwest Outfitters)

[00:02:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on competitive positioning from our last session.

[00:05:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:10:00] David (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:14:00] David (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:17:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] David (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:26:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:29:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:31:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:35:00] David (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-L trap (No due-dates extracted when transcript mentioned timing); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-L failure mode (No due-dates extracted when transcript mentioned timing)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-L -- No due-dates extracted when transcript mentioned timing
